commands.review_summary:
The branch updates Slack native approval target resolution and forwarding fallback suppression for app-conversation plugin approvals, with focused Slack docs, changelog, and tests.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is clear: current main can reinterpret Slack app-conversation `D...` turn-source targets as user routes and compares Slack thread ids by leading epoch. I did not execute runtime tests in this read-only sweep.
commands.review_followup: Human maintainer review remains appropriate because the protected `maintainer` label and Slack routing compatibility risk need an owner decision; no narrow automated repair is currently identified.

target.title: fix(slack): keep plugin approvals in app conversation threads
target.body:
## Summary

- preserve Slack app-conversation `D...` turn-source targets as concrete `channel:D...` native approval destinations
- require exact Slack thread timestamp matches before treating app-thread `channel:D...` and stored `user:U...` routes as compatible
- make plugin forwarding fallback suppression target-aware so explicit Slack plugin targets are not hidden by native origin handling
- clarify Slack docs around native approval-client delivery versus `approvals.plugin`

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/slack/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/threading-tool-context.test.ts extensions/slack/src/send.blocks.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/provider.allowlist.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/elicitation-bridge.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm format:check docs/channels/slack.md docs/gateway/config-channels.md docs/tools/exec-approvals-advanced.md extensions/slack/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts extensions/slack/src/approval-native-gates.ts extensions/slack/src/approval-native.ts extensions/slack/src/approval-native.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:docs:check docs/channels/slack.md docs/gateway/config-channels.md docs/tools/exec-approvals-advanced.md`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/slack/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts extensions/slack/src/approval-native-gates.ts extensions/slack/src/approval-native.ts extensions/slack/src/approval-native.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `uvx showboat verify /Users/kevinlin/code/openclaw/.mem/main/proofs/demo-20-dev-slack-app-plugin-approval-routing/raw/showboat-summary.md`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: native Slack plugin approvals that originate from a Slack app conversation now target the originating app-conversation thread instead of falling back to a separate Slack surface.

Real environment tested: local dev Slack profile with the gateway restarted from `codex/slack-app-conversation-plugin-approval-routing-main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: called `plugin.approval.request` through the dev gateway with Slack app-conversation source facts (`turnSourceTo = D0AEMJ2SM55`, `turnSourceThreadId = 1779399056.858309`), queried Slack `conversations.replies`, called `plugin.approval.resolve` with `deny`, and queried the same thread again.

Evidence after fix: Slack API found exactly one matching approval message at `1779412291.148939` in thread `1779399056.858309` with Block Kit actions `Allow Once` and `Deny`; after resolution the same message remained in the same thread with no actions block.

Observed result after fix: the live native plugin approval runtime posted and resolved the approval in the original Slack app-conversation thread.

What was not tested: the full Computer Use elicitation and Slack screenshot path was blocked because the desktop was locked; a direct `chat.send` attempt with explicit Slack origin fields was also rejected by the gateway admin-scope guard, so this PR records the full user-path UI proof as a gap rather than claiming it passed.
target.author: kevinslin

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
